surfaced_from: PWA request 2026-05-25 05:19 UTC — CISSP-like CTO security audit and prioritization
capability_needed: Quarantine or retire public clone candidates immediately after validation windows
details: Hetzner currently shows multiple CTO-related public servers, including two clone-candidate/v2 servers created today with public IPv4/IPv6 addresses, no cloud firewall, no backup window, and no delete/rebuild protection. Test-mode labels reduce self-replacement risk but not network exposure.
search_trail: Ran openclaw status and openclaw security audit --deep on cto-v1; result: 0 critical, 1 warn, 1 info; gateway bound to 127.0.0.1 with token auth.
Ran ss -ltnup on cto-v1; found public SSH plus public HTTP/HTTPS and loopback-only OpenClaw/Hermes/A2A services.
Queried Hetzner API for servers, primary IPs, and firewalls; found no cloud firewalls and multiple public CTO servers/candidates.
Inspected /etc/caddy/Caddyfile without secrets; cto.husband.llc reverse-proxies to 127.0.0.1:8088 with security headers and access logging.
Scanned git history/current tree for secret-shaped patterns and key material; confirmed historical VAPID private key material and multiple documented credential-handling risks.
Checked file permissions for /opt/cto, ~/.openclaw, ~/.hermes, ~/.codex; sensitive home config dirs are mostly 0600/0700, while project files are group-writable by cto group.
Checked unattended-upgrades; service is enabled and active.
Attempted privileged sshd/fail2ban verification; elevated execution is unavailable in this PWA runtime, so privileged SSH posture remains unverified.
proposed_resolution: Define candidate lifecycle controls: default firewall quarantine, explicit TTL, auto-destroy on failed/expired validation, labels required for cleanup, and no public PWA binding until promotion.
blast_radius: Aggressive cleanup could destroy useful evidence or a candidate John still needs to test.
rollback_plan: Preserve handoff/evidence first; if cleanup was premature, rebuild from the recorded strategy or snapshot.
resolution_notes:
2026-05-27T07:50Z OpenClaw pump: performed non-destructive Hetzner clone-candidate exposure sweep. Hetzner API listed two running servers: `cto-v1` (production CTO, labels purpose=cto/version=v1) and `recrm` (non-CTO project). No `cto-clone`, `candidate`, `test`, or unlabeled CTO clone server was present, so there is no disposable candidate to quarantine/retire in this tick. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-008-clone-candidate-sweep-2026-05-27T0750Z.md.

2026-05-27T19:35Z OpenClaw pump: added a guarded failed clone-candidate retirement watchdog after the required A2A2H no-drift check. `scripts/security/clone-candidate-watchdog.py` reads preserved clone failure summaries and live Hetzner inventory, defaults to dry-run, and will only delete with `--destroy` when the server has the exact safe labels `purpose=cto`, `role=clone-candidate`, `test_mode=true`; production-label mismatches block deletion. Live dry-run showed the previously reported failed candidate `132825157` is already absent from Hetzner inventory, so nothing was destroyed. Added regression coverage in `tests/test_clone_candidate_watchdog.py` and wired it into `scripts/validate-no-spend.sh`. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-008-clone-candidate-watchdog-2026-05-27T1935Z.md.

2026-05-27T20:15Z OpenClaw pump: closed the remaining operational gap by wiring failed-candidate retirement into scripts/install.sh itself. After Hetzner provisioning, the wrapper now preserves a failure summary under logs/clone/candidates/ and requests Hetzner deletion on wrapper failure; if deletion fails, scripts/security/clone-candidate-watchdog.py can retry from that summary. Added tests/test_install_wrapper_candidate_retirement.py. Verification passed: bash -n scripts/install.sh; python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_clone_candidate_watchdog.py tests/test_install_wrapper_candidate_retirement.py (6 passed); bash scripts/validate-no-spend.sh; live watchdog dry run still reports prior server 132825157 already_absent. Artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-008-install-wrapper-autoretire-2026-05-27T2015Z.md.
